beats.narration: Your flaw detector measures only time and voltage. Standardization converts them into distance and amplitude you can act on.

payload.title: STANDARDIZATION
payload.sub: Teaching the instrument what distance and amplitude mean.

beats.narration: Range first. Repeat echoes from a 25 millimeter block must land on 25, 50, 75, 100. Same shift? Zero error. Wrong spacing? Velocity.

payload.heading: Range: teach the time base distance
payload.note: SAME SHIFT = ZERO · WRONG SPACING = VELOCITY

beats.narration: Sensitivity: peak a known reflector, set it to eighty percent of full screen height, and record that gain. That is your reference level.

chips.t: Set it to 80% full screen height

beats.narration: That reflector lives in a reference block: the I I W for exit point, angle, range; flat-bottom holes for amplitude; side-drilled holes for the DAC.
Standardization is perishable, so verify on schedule. Fail the final check, and everything since the last good check gets done again. Standardize, then scan.

payload.sub: Fail the final check, and you re-inspect back to the last good one.
